race.name: Jämtland Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Sixty kilometers through the deep forests of inland Sweden — Jämtland's quiet answer to the coastal crowds.

climate.description: Jämtland's interior location produces cold, stable winters with reliable snow cover. Continental climate means colder temperatures than coastal areas, with calm conditions in the forested valleys.
challenges: Cold temperatures in sheltered forest valleys
Wax selection in cold, dry conditions

course.primary: 60km through the dense boreal forests of inland Jämtland. Rolling terrain with long forested stretches and occasional lake crossings. A wilderness experience with minimal spectator access along much of the route.
course.surface: Groomed classic tracks through forest and lake terrain
features: Minimal spectator access creates solitary experience

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A genuine wilderness marathon with beautiful forest scenery, but limited prestige, small field, and remote location hold back the overall score.

history.summary: The Jämtland Ski Marathon is a 60km classic race through the interior forests of Jämtland, one of Sweden's most sparsely populated regions. The race showcases the deep boreal wilderness of inland Scandinavia, offering a dramatically different experience from the crowded Vasaloppet corridor further south.
notable_facts: Traverses some of Sweden's most remote boreal forest
Jämtland is one of Sweden's least populated regions
Offers a wilderness alternative to more commercial Swedish loppets
Course passes through terrain frequented by moose, lynx, and wolverine
